codeReview.summary: The cli-framework feature is well-implemented. Commander.js CLI with init/config/set-agent commands, config file handling at ~/.mission-board/config.json, agent ID validation, and standalone binary compilation are all working correctly. Tests cover config directory creation, path resolution, default config generation, existing config loading, config saving, and agent ID validation (12 tests total, all passing). The binary compiles and runs successfully.

issues.description: Unused constants CONFIG_DIR and CONFIG_FILE at the top of the test file. These reference homedir() but are never used — the tests use testConfigDir and testConfigFile instead. Dead code from initial scaffolding.

Uses node:fs/promises (mkdir, readFile, writeFile, access) instead of Bun.file/Bun.write as recommended in CLAUDE.md coding guidelines. This is functionally correct since Bun fully supports Node APIs, but is a minor convention deviation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: CLI worker uses node:fs/promises instead of Bun.file for file I/O, deviating from the project's CLAUDE.md guidelines ('Prefer Bun.file over node:fs readFile/writeFile'). AGENTS.md does not explicitly reinforce this for CLI code. Later CLI feature workers followed the same node:fs/promises pattern set by this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cli/src/config.ts:1 — import { mkdir, readFile, writeFile, access } from 'node:fs/promises'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The cli-worker skill's work procedure step 1 says 'Write tests first (RED)' but the transcript skeleton shows the worker read mission docs and AGENTS.md before writing any code, which is appropriate startup behavior. The worker did follow the TDD-like approach of writing tests and implementation. Skill procedure matched reality well.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: Read mission.md → Read validation-contract.md → Read package.json → Read AGENTS.md → Read services.yaml → LS src/ → LS root → LS tests/ → Glob src/**/*.ts → Read tsconfig.json. Then implementation proceeded.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The build:cli command was already present in .factory/services.yaml at the time of this feature's implementation, confirming good state tracking.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml lists 'build:cli: bun run build:cli' under commands.

summary: The cli-framework feature is a solid implementation that meets all four expected behaviors: config auto-creation on first run, agent ID prompt and storage, binary compilation to ./dist/mission, and standalone binary execution. Code quality is good with proper error handling and validation. Two minor non-blocking issues noted: unused constants in test file and a convention deviation using node:fs/promises instead of Bun.file. All 12 tests pass, typecheck passes, and binary compilation succeeds. Status: PASS.
